title: Paginated KV demultiplexer and prefill quiet-window to reduce thrash
preview: Problem: Paged attention workloads experience periodic throughput regressions when large numbers of cold tenants or long sequences trigger aggressive full-slot prefill and eviction churn. This manifests as increased tail latency, KV cache fragmentation, and suboptimal kernel selection decisions (the kernel selector ...
text:
Problem: Paged attention workloads experience periodic throughput regressions when large numbers of cold tenants or long sequences trigger aggressive full-slot prefill and eviction churn. This manifests as increased tail latency, KV cache fragmentation, and suboptimal kernel selection decisions (the kernel selector flips between fast small-kernel and fallback large-kernel under thrash), especially during cold-start/failure recovery.

Proposal: Implement a 'paginated KV demultiplexer' that separates incoming prefill requests into two admission classes (fast-path micro-prefill and background full-prefill) based on estimated decode cost, kernel affinity, and KV slab granularity. Add a 'prefill quiet-window' admission policy: after a slab receives a micro-prefill it gains a short quiet window where background compaction/eviction is deprioritized to avoid immediate re-eviction on follow-up...

title: session-pool-ballooning-and-prefix-cache-pointer-race-runtime-fix
preview: Summary: Multiple customers (Hosted burst pools and a Private deployment for a regulated customer) reported sudden GPU OOM spikes followed by degraded throughput. Investigation shows a previously unobserved race between session pool growth (we've named it 'ballooning') and the prefix-store tombstone sweep which caus...
text:
Summary: Multiple customers (Hosted burst pools and a Private deployment for a regulated customer) reported sudden GPU OOM spikes followed by degraded throughput. Investigation shows a previously unobserved race between session pool growth (we've named it 'ballooning') and the prefix-store tombstone sweep which caused stale pointer chains to accumulate in the prefix cache metadata, preventing GC and causing page saturation on GPU arenas. The failure surface included increased allocator retention, long-lived session objects pinned by expired prefix entries, and cascading OOMs under bursty traffic patterns.

Impact: Incident affected three customer environments intermittently over the past 36 hours: two hosted burst pools (short latency spikes then OOMs) and one private cluster where a single tenant experienced sustained OOMs and service degradation for ~45 minutes during peak. Traffic ...

title: Cost Investigator component bridge and interaction audit
preview: Audit and design bridging components for the new Cost Investigator surface: an exploratory workspace in the Redwood Console that helps customers investigate anomalous spend, correlate traces to model routes, and run quick what-if simulations. This ticket covers component composition, token mapping for contextual sta...
text: Audit and design bridging components for the new Cost Investigator surface: an exploratory workspace in the Redwood Console that helps customers investigate anomalous spend, correlate traces to model routes, and run quick what-if simulations. This ticket covers component composition, token mapping for contextual states, interactive behaviors (hover/expand/focus), and handoff artifacts for engineering and product. Goal is a single, composable component library subset that can be reused in the dashboard, rollout inspector, and timeline comparison views.

title: Coordinated artifact pinsets and driver-compat orchestration for safe upgrades
preview:
Background:
Redwood must ensure safe, auditable upgrades of inference nodes and runtime images while keeping per-customer SLAs. Today we have SBOMs and image tags but lack a coordinated artifact pinset and driver compatibility orchestration that ties together: container image, signed SBOM, CUDA/driver pin, and hardw...
text:
Background:
Redwood must ensure safe, auditable upgrades of inference nodes and runtime images while keeping per-customer SLAs. Today we have SBOMs and image tags but lack a coordinated artifact pinset and driver compatibility orchestration that ties together: container image, signed SBOM, CUDA/driver pin, and hardware fingerprint. That gap causes manual work during LTS upgrades and blocks fast rollouts for Dedicated/Private fleets.

Goal:
- Define and implement a canonical artifact pinset manifest (pinset.json) that binds: container image digest, signed SBOM reference, kernel/CUDA/driver versions, and optional hardware capabilities.
- Provide an orchestration service + CLI (working name: artifact-orch) to compute, validate, and roll pinsets through staged rollout gates (staging canary, perf smoke, hardware-compat gate, full rollout).
- Integrate pinsets with existing rollout engine s...

title: Contain latency regression with per-customer hedging and adaptive microbatch scaling
preview: Multiple enterprise customers reported sudden increases in TTFT (first-token latency) and degraded tokens/sec across Hosted and Dedicated deployments when certain request mixes hit mixed-precision Llama variants. Impact spikes started 2026-02-28 22:00 UTC and correlated with a scheduler change rolled 2026-02-28 that...
text: Multiple enterprise customers reported sudden increases in TTFT (first-token latency) and degraded tokens/sec across Hosted and Dedicated deployments when certain request mixes hit mixed-precision Llama variants. Impact spikes started 2026-02-28 22:00 UTC and correlated with a scheduler change rolled 2026-02-28 that altered microbatch handshake timeouts and kernel selection thresholds. This ticket contains an emergency containment plan, implemented mitigations, measurements, and follow-ups to prevent recurrence and restore SLOs.

title: Econ Slicer Engine for Multi-Tenant Route Profiling
preview: Build a backend service (Econ Slicer) that computes per-route / per-model / per-customer cost and latency breakdowns for the Console and downstream billing/chargeback pipelines. The service must produce deterministic, explainable attributions for batched and streaming requests, account for KV-cache reuse and prefix ...
text: Build a backend service (Econ Slicer) that computes per-route / per-model / per-customer cost and latency breakdowns for the Console and downstream billing/chargeback pipelines. The service must produce deterministic, explainable attributions for batched and streaming requests, account for KV-cache reuse and prefix caching, and surface confidence scores when fallbacks or multi-model routing occur. Outputs must be queryable by the Console (near-real-time) and consumable by the billing ingestion pipeline (hourly batch). The system should prioritize correctness and auditability, with configurable aggregation windows and deterministic tie-breaking rules so finance and customers can reconcile billed amounts against runtime traces.

title: Trajectory & Autoscale Experiments Cadence — Quarterly Roadmap and Runbook
preview: This ticket defines the Q2 cadence and prioritized slate of experiments for improving Dedicated capacity economics via adaptive autoscale and predictive trajectory windows. Goal is to produce a 10-week experimentation plan plus an operations runbook that can be handed to Capacity Ops and SRE for execution and to lin...
text: This ticket defines the Q2 cadence and prioritized slate of experiments for improving Dedicated capacity economics via adaptive autoscale and predictive trajectory windows. Goal is to produce a 10-week experimentation plan plus an operations runbook that can be handed to Capacity Ops and SRE for execution and to link clear OKRs and success metrics back to billing and SLOs. This initiative should align with Console signals (cost per token, prewarm hit-rate) and Runtime telemetry (cold-start latency, queue depth).

title: SLO Arbitration Fabric: proportional soft-reservations & transient KV slab leasing for continuous batching
preview: Problem: The current continuous batching system uses hard preemption heuristics that either over-evict KV cache (hurting long-context workloads) or favor throughput-heavy tenants at the expense of latency SLOs for small interactive tenants. Goals: 1) Implement an SLO Arbitration Fabric (SAF) that enforces per-tenant...
text: Problem: The current continuous batching system uses hard preemption heuristics that either over-evict KV cache (hurting long-context workloads) or favor throughput-heavy tenants at the expense of latency SLOs for small interactive tenants. Goals: 1) Implement an SLO Arbitration Fabric (SAF) that enforces per-tenant latency budgets while preserving aggregate GPU utilization; 2) Introduce proportional soft-reservations for batch slots and transient KV slab leases to avoid hard eviction storms; 3) Provide a predictable degradation path that trades small throughput drops for latency guarantees; 4) Integrate monitoring, simulation, and rollout plan to validate safety before full rollout.

title: Onramp Snapshot Gallery: compact latency / cost / trace tiles for Quickstart
preview: Create a compact, exportable gallery of observability screenshots for the Quickstart flow: three-tile landing (latency distribution, cost-per-token sparkline, request trace waterfall), a single expanded trace view with hover callouts, and a dark/light variant set for docs and in-product help. Deliverables: Figma sou...
text: Create a compact, exportable gallery of observability screenshots for the Quickstart flow: three-tile landing (latency distribution, cost-per-token sparkline, request trace waterfall), a single expanded trace view with hover callouts, and a dark/light variant set for docs and in-product help. Deliverables: Figma source, annotated PNG/WEBP exports at 2x and 3x, redacted sample datasets, and a handover note describing where to swap real telemetry. The assets will be used in the Quickstart doc, the onboarding modal, and the Console release notes.

title: Implement Dedicated GPU-seconds meter v2 (idle + autoscaling transition definitions)
preview:
## Context
We’ve had repeated confusion (and at least one customer escalation) around Dedicated usage reporting because our current GPU-hours/GPU-seconds logic does not have crisp, implementable rules for:
- idle time vs “available capacity” vs “in-use capacity”
- autoscaling transitions (pool scale-up/scale-down, i...
text:
## Context
We’ve had repeated confusion (and at least one customer escalation) around Dedicated usage reporting because our current GPU-hours/GPU-seconds logic does not have crisp, implementable rules for:
- idle time vs “available capacity” vs “in-use capacity”
- autoscaling transitions (pool scale-up/scale-down, instance drains)
- partial-hour rounding differences between exports vs invoice line items

This ticket implements **GPU-seconds Meter v2** for Dedicated so Finance/RevRec can reconcile usage -> ledger consistently and so Customer Success can explain usage to customers without “it depends on autoscaling” handwaving.

This is part of the broader metering hardening initiative (shadow billing, drift checks, and month-end close operationalization).

## Goal
Ship a v2 metering pipeline for Dedicated that:
1) matches the **formal meter definition** (see Confluence link)
2) provide...

title: Dedicated: per-tenant/cluster batching tier overrides + precedence rules
preview:
Dedicated customers need deterministic control of batching tier/profile selection with clear override precedence.

Implement:
- Per-tenant override: ability for Dedicated tenants to force a batching tier (fast|standard|cost_efficient) and/or explicit batching knob overrides (where allowed) at the tenant/cluster leve...
text:
Dedicated customers need deterministic control of batching tier/profile selection with clear override precedence.

Implement:
- Per-tenant override: ability for Dedicated tenants to force a batching tier (fast|standard|cost_efficient) and/or explicit batching knob overrides (where allowed) at the tenant/cluster level.
- Cluster override: ability for Redwood ops to pin a cluster-level default tier for a Dedicated cluster when capacity characteristics differ (GPU type mix, MIG, etc.).
- Precedence definition (must be documented and enforced consistently across control plane + runtime):
 1) Customer override (tenant/cluster level in Dedicated) 
 2) Route policy tier selection (route/model policy configuration)
 3) Tier default selected by workload-aware logic (runtime heuristics)

Non-goals (for this ticket):
- Per-request overrides for Dedicated (we may add later; risk of abuse/high car...

title: Policy engine: add auto-rollback config schema, validation, and migration for rollout policies
preview:
Context
We need to extend the control plane policy engine schema to support SLO-driven auto-actions during rollouts (pause and rollback). This ticket covers:
1) schema changes (policy/config) that describe thresholds/windows/guardrails
2) validation (static + cross-field) with safe bounds to prevent flapping/unsafe ...
text:
Context
We need to extend the control plane policy engine schema to support SLO-driven auto-actions during rollouts (pause and rollback). This ticket covers:
1) schema changes (policy/config) that describe thresholds/windows/guardrails
2) validation (static + cross-field) with safe bounds to prevent flapping/unsafe configs
3) migration plan for existing rollout policies so default behavior is unchanged (auto-rollback disabled unless explicitly enabled)

Goals
- Define canonical policy shape for auto-actions on SLO breach, aligned with PRD + design spec and ADR-0127.
- Ensure misconfigured policies fail fast with actionable error messages in Console/API.
- Provide an explicit migration plan for existing stored rollout policies, including versioning/defaulting.

Non-goals
- Controller-side evaluation logic (ENG-4821 / ENG-4861).
- Audit event schema and event emission (ADR-0128, ENG-483...

title: Implement tiered batching profile config schema + validation (incl. migration)
preview:
Goal
Ship a backwards-compatible config schema for three batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient), plus validation rules and a migration plan for existing manual batching configs.

Context
We’re introducing tiered defaults that map to scheduler knobs. The runtime needs a clear, stable configuration contract...
text:
Goal
Ship a backwards-compatible config schema for three batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient), plus validation rules and a migration plan for existing manual batching configs.

Context
We’re introducing tiered defaults that map to scheduler knobs. The runtime needs a clear, stable configuration contract that:
- works across Hosted API, Dedicated, and Private deployments
- supports route-level policy selection and per-tenant overrides (Dedicated)
- prevents foot-guns (conflicting knobs, impossible combinations, accidental global tier changes)
- supports safe rollout (feature flag, kill switch, and easy rollback)

Scope (this ticket)
1) Define the config schema (control plane + runtime consumption)
2) Implement validation + precedence rules
3) Provide migration behavior for existing configs
4) Add docs/notes for downstream integrators (Console + SDKs) on the schema contract...

title: Introduce adaptive semantic palette and container-aware elevation system
preview: Problem: Our current token set has grown organically and exhibits visual inconsistencies in multi-component contexts: chart palettes feel disconnected from UI accent ramps, table row dividers clash with elevation steps, and dense components (compact tables, small buttons) use the same radius and shadow values as lar...
text:
Problem: Our current token set has grown organically and exhibits visual inconsistencies in multi-component contexts: chart palettes feel disconnected from UI accent ramps, table row dividers clash with elevation steps, and dense components (compact tables, small buttons) use the same radius and shadow values as large container components, causing visual noise.

Goal: Create an adaptive semantic color palette (named tonal anchors) that maps to functional roles (bg-surface, bg-muted, accent-primary, accent-critical, success, info, neutral-ink) with light/dark variants tuned for both UI chrome and data viz. Introduce a container-aware elevation system that scales shadow/elevation micro-steps based on container scale (compact | default | expansive) so small controls keep subtle separation while large panels maintain depth. Update typography scale to reconcile dense layouts and dataviz la...

title: Support marketplace multi-subscription linking edge cases (existing orgs, re-subscribe, entitlement delays)
preview:
Problem
Marketplace buyers are hitting brittle behavior when (a) an existing Redwood org subscribes again, (b) a single Redwood org ends up with multiple marketplace subscriptions (base SKU + add-ons, or multiple listings), and (c) entitlements are not immediately visible due to AWS/GCP propagation delays. Today the...
text:
Problem
Marketplace buyers are hitting brittle behavior when (a) an existing Redwood org subscribes again, (b) a single Redwood org ends up with multiple marketplace subscriptions (base SKU + add-ons, or multiple listings), and (c) entitlements are not immediately visible due to AWS/GCP propagation delays. Today the linking flow assumes a near 1:1:1 relationship (marketplace subscription -> Redwood org -> effective entitlements), which breaks in real procurement and re-procurement scenarios.

This ticket hardens the control-plane linking + entitlement evaluation behavior so the console onboarding flow can be idempotent, safe, and support multi-subscription semantics without creating duplicate orgs or inconsistent billing/limits.

Scope
1) Existing org subscribes again (re-subscribe)
- If the marketplace customer identifier is already linked to an org, the linking operation must be ide...

title: Harden marketplace entitlement sync job (retries, idempotency, alerting)
preview:
Context
Marketplace buyers (AWS + GCP) are hitting provisioning delays after subscribing because our entitlement sync is occasionally failing silently, retrying too slowly, or producing inconsistent results across repeated runs. This directly impacts the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning funnel and has b...
text:
Context
Marketplace buyers (AWS + GCP) are hitting provisioning delays after subscribing because our entitlement sync is occasionally failing silently, retrying too slowly, or producing inconsistent results across repeated runs. This directly impacts the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning funnel and has been a recurring escalation path (see SUP-4821).

Goal
Improve reliability and debuggability of the entitlement sync job:
- Faster and safer recovery from transient partner/API issues (retries + backoff)
- Idempotency so repeated sync runs do not create duplicate entitlements / churn state
- Strong alerting and runbook to reduce time-to-detect/resolve

Scope
Applies to:
- Scheduled entitlement sync job (poll + reconcile)
- On-demand sync trigger used by console onboarding retry flows
- AWS Marketplace Entitlement / Metering integration and GCP Marketplace procurement entitle...

title: Oscillating prefetch orchestrator for mixed-length continuous scheduler tuning
preview: Problem: Mixed prompt/response workloads (web chat + API summarization) cause the continuous-batcher to oscillate between aggressive prefetching and conservative slot allocation. That oscillation increases GPU memory churn and KV cache fragmentation, resulting in 10-25% tail latency spikes and up to 12% higher memor...
text:
Problem: Mixed prompt/response workloads (web chat + API summarization) cause the continuous-batcher to oscillate between aggressive prefetching and conservative slot allocation. That oscillation increases GPU memory churn and KV cache fragmentation, resulting in 10-25% tail latency spikes and up to 12% higher memory pressure on p4d nodes in production.

Goal: Reduce oscillation and stabilize KV residency while preserving user-facing latency SLOs for short-turn interactive flows and long-turn generation. Implement a scheduler-level damping mechanism ("oscillation damping") and a KV bank rebalancer that proactively coils prefetched KV into compact banks to reduce fragmentation during bursts. Validate on stage with synthetic mixed-length traces and a small customer synthetic traffic test.

Context: We've tuned length-aware batching and prefetch windows independently, but the coupling be...

title: Marketplace entitlement verification API (AWS/GCP) for subscription → org provisioning flow
preview:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

T...
text:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

This ticket implements/extends control-plane API endpoints used by the marketplace onboarding entrypoint to:
1) verify a buyer’s AWS/GCP marketplace subscription is active and mapped to a Redwood SKU
2) return a stable entitlement object (or a clear, retryable error) that Console + provisioning can act on
3) support edge cases (existing org linking, multiple subscriptions, delayed propagation)

This is a critical dependency for the marketplace refresh launch (time-to-first-token + reduced support escalations).

## Goals / Non-goals
### Goals
- Provide a single verification A...

title: Usage Insights beta: feature flag + account allowlist + admin enablement workflow
preview:
## Summary
Implement targeted enablement for the Usage Insights beta (route+model breakdown + exports) using a feature flag and an account/workspace allowlist, with an internal admin toggle + a rollout checklist.

This ticket is specifically about gating and operational enablement (who sees the feature and how we tu...
text:
## Summary
Implement targeted enablement for the Usage Insights beta (route+model breakdown + exports) using a feature flag and an account/workspace allowlist, with an internal admin toggle + a rollout checklist.

This ticket is specifically about gating and operational enablement (who sees the feature and how we turn it on safely), not the feature UI itself.

## Background / Why
We need a controlled beta rollout for Usage Insights:
- Ensure only selected workspaces/accounts can access the feature while we validate correctness vs billing and performance.
- Avoid accidental exposure of cost data to customers/workspaces that have cost views disabled.
- Provide a repeatable internal workflow for AEs/SEs/CSM to request enablement (INT tickets) and for Eng/SRE to rollout gradually.

Related artifacts:
- Beta candidate list (Drive): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/product/console-and-rol...

title: Starter Flow micro-screens and contextual screenshots for Optimize: batching, KV cache, and quantization presets
preview: Create a compact set of starter UX assets and product screenshots to live in the Optimize quickstart and release notes. Focus on three core surfaces: 1) Quick presets (Cost-first vs Latency-first vs Balanced) with microcopy explaining trade-offs, 2) Inline UI for per-route quantization presets and a summary chip sho...
text: Create a compact set of starter UX assets and product screenshots to live in the Optimize quickstart and release notes. Focus on three core surfaces: 1) Quick presets (Cost-first vs Latency-first vs Balanced) with microcopy explaining trade-offs, 2) Inline UI for per-route quantization presets and a summary chip showing expected token cost delta, 3) KV-cache visual explainer with a toggle and animated prefix caching micro-screenshot. Assets should be designed for small hero placements in the Console, quickstart modal, and documentation thumbnails. Deliverables: Figma screens, a library of annotated PNGs for docs (desktop and mobile widths), micro-animations/GIF prototypes for the caching toggle, and alt text + microcopy suggestions.

title: Implement Region Health Aggregator Service + integrate into routing signal pipeline
preview:
## Context
Today failover triggers are split across multiple systems (burn alerts, ad-hoc checks, some routing policies) and are hard to validate safely. We need a single, well-defined **Region Health** signal that can be consumed by routing/policy evaluation and by the failover controller.

This ticket implements a...
text:
## Context
Today failover triggers are split across multiple systems (burn alerts, ad-hoc checks, some routing policies) and are hard to validate safely. We need a single, well-defined **Region Health** signal that can be consumed by routing/policy evaluation and by the failover controller.

This ticket implements a **Region Health Aggregator Service** (control-plane owned) that ingests multiple signal streams, computes a deterministic health state per region (and per "route" where relevant), and exposes an API for routing to consume as a first-class signal.

Primary goals:
- Provide a single authoritative, low-latency region health view to routing/policy engine.
- Make signal evaluation deterministic and debuggable (what inputs caused UNHEALTHY?).
- Prevent stampedes and noisy decisioning via caching/TTL + dampening at the service boundary.

Non-goals:
- This service does not execute...

title: Feature-flag targeting + holdout mechanism for console onboarding nudges
preview:
Goal
Implement a deterministic cohort assignment + targeting layer for in-console onboarding nudges so Growth/Product can run controlled experiments (A/B + holdout) without custom code per nudge.

Context
Lifecycle growth experiments sprint is running multiple nudge tests (API key creation, first request, enable str...
text:
Goal
Implement a deterministic cohort assignment + targeting layer for in-console onboarding nudges so Growth/Product can run controlled experiments (A/B + holdout) without custom code per nudge.

Context
Lifecycle growth experiments sprint is running multiple nudge tests (API key creation, first request, enable streaming, view usage dashboard). We need:
- Targeting by cohort (experiment assignment), signup date window, and region
- A holdout group that sees NO nudges (or sees baseline nudges) to measure incremental lift
- Exposure logging so analytics can do SRM checks and downstream funnel attribution

Requirements
1) Deterministic assignment
- Assign each eligible user to one of: holdout | control | variant(s)
- Assignment must be stable across sessions/devices (same user always gets same bucket)
- Assignment must be independent per experiment (user can be holdout in one experiment...

title: Canary rollout controller for Optimization Config safe apply (percent/shard) with pause/resume + gating hooks
preview:
Context
- We are introducing versioned Optimization Config objects and a safe apply workflow. This ticket is the rollout controller responsible for staged application (percent-based and/or shard-based) and for wiring gating hooks (latency, 5xx, saturation, and optional eval quality gates).
- This is the “brains” of ...
text:
Context
- We are introducing versioned Optimization Config objects and a safe apply workflow. This ticket is the rollout controller responsible for staged application (percent-based and/or shard-based) and for wiring gating hooks (latency, 5xx, saturation, and optional eval quality gates).
- This is the “brains” of safe apply: it drives the apply forward, pauses on risk, and ensures we can resume or abort deterministically.

Goal / outcome
- Operators can apply an Optimization Config version to a scope (tenant/model/region) using a staged rollout strategy (canary → progressive ramp → full).
- Each stage must evaluate gates and either proceed, pause, or fail the rollout with a clear reason.
- Must support pause/resume, and be resilient to retries and process restarts.

Non-goals (for initial GA)
- Fully automated rollback without human confirmation for all customers.
- Multi-config tra...
tags: Safe apply + rollback mechanism (Optimization Config versioning)

title: gpu-instance-pool-orchestrator-module-for-private-autoscaling-and-upgrades
preview: Goal: Provide a single Terraform module that operators can use in Private (VPC) deployments to provision and manage GPU instance pools with multi-mode autoscaling: Karpenter as primary provisioner and ASG as a durable fallback. The module must offer a safe upgrade path for GPU drivers and instance types and include ...
text:
Goal: Provide a single Terraform module that operators can use in Private (VPC) deployments to provision and manage GPU instance pools with multi-mode autoscaling: Karpenter as primary provisioner and ASG as a durable fallback. The module must offer a safe upgrade path for GPU drivers and instance types and include automated node health checks specific to GPUs so that degraded nodes are remediated promptly.

Scope and requirements:
- Terraform module with clear inputs/outputs, semantic versioning, and examples for: Karpenter-only, ASG-only, and hybrid (Karpenter primary + ASG fallback) setups inside customer VPCs.
- Support configurable GPU instance selectors (g4/g5/a10), instance lifecycle policies, taints/labels for Redwood runtime scheduling, and spot vs on-demand mix.
- Rolling upgrade capability: ability to do controlled instance type or AMI updates with pre-drain hooks and verif...

title: Adaptive setpoint autoscale soak probe (reverse-sawtooth + steady-state)
preview: Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-...
text: Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-start incidents, and KV cache hit ratio; detect regressions vs a rolling baseline; and produce signals suitable for integration with our autoscaler fuzzing and alerting pipelines. Unlike previous burst/soak harnesses, this probe uses a thermostatic controller to nudge target capacity when p95 drifts above configurable thresholds, emulating an operator-adjusted with hysteresis to avoid oscillation.

title: Correlated Failure Matrix + Payload Diff Lens — prototype for multi-request root-cause mapping
preview: Goal: Design a compact, scannable Correlated Failure Matrix that surfaces cross-request correlations (shared stack frames, common model variants, identical input prefixes) and an embedded Payload Diff Lens that highlights minimal textual/structured diffs between successful and failed calls. Intended for mid-to-senio...
text: Goal: Design a compact, scannable Correlated Failure Matrix that surfaces cross-request correlations (shared stack frames, common model variants, identical input prefixes) and an embedded Payload Diff Lens that highlights minimal textual/structured diffs between successful and failed calls. Intended for mid-to-senior engineers and SREs who triage production regressions in the Redwood Console. Scope: Matrix view as entrypoint from failed request list; shows clusters of related failures with correlation signals and confidence scores. Drill-in Payload Diff Lens shows token-level diffs, JSON path diffs for structured outputs, and quick-run replays with suggested mitigations. Lightweight timeline and linkouts to request traces, logs, and rerun tools are included. Non-goals: full session replay studio and probabilistic root-cause inference (we only show correlation signals and explainabilit...

title: Density-first Console cards + table/ chart fallbacks for cross-browser polish
preview: Problem: Several Console pages (dashboards, cost view, rollouts) have inconsistent density and breakpoints across browsers. On Safari and older Edge builds charts overflow, modal stacks create clipping with fixed-position elements, and table row wrapping causes a poor reading rhythm at small widths. Goal: Deliver a ...
text: Problem: Several Console pages (dashboards, cost view, rollouts) have inconsistent density and breakpoints across browsers. On Safari and older Edge builds charts overflow, modal stacks create clipping with fixed-position elements, and table row wrapping causes a poor reading rhythm at small widths. Goal: Deliver a density-first responsive system that preserves readability, accessibility, and consistent behaviour across Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge. This ticket covers the design prototypes, interaction specs, and a set of cross-browser fallbacks for charts, tables, and modals so engineering can implement without regressions.

title: Disconnected-client heuristics and partial-output fanout cleanup
preview: Problem: When clients disconnect mid-generation we occasionally see duplicated partial payloads, lingering per-generation state in memory, and cross-tenant KV cache pollution during 'fanout' (when a single generation is mirrored to multiple downstream consumers). This causes elevated memory usage, confusing logs tha...
text:
Problem: When clients disconnect mid-generation we occasionally see duplicated partial payloads, lingering per-generation state in memory, and cross-tenant KV cache pollution during 'fanout' (when a single generation is mirrored to multiple downstream consumers). This causes elevated memory usage, confusing logs that look like duplicate outputs, and complicates function/tool-call coordination for resumed sessions.

Goal: Implement a deterministic, idempotent cleanup and resume protocol that: (1) detects and cordons disconnected clients quickly, (2) marks partial generations with a compact emission fingerprint (EID) to prevent duplicate downstream fanout, (3) quarantines orphaned generation state in a TTL-based soft-quarantine for safe rollback/inspection, and (4) exposes clear metrics and alerts for stale-generation accumulation.

Constraints: Must be backward-compatible with existing...

title: Hierarchical workload sieve + safe-splay rollout for automated batching recommendations
preview: Build a recommendation engine that profiles workloads at multiple granularities (route, model, customer cohort, temporal windows) using a 'hierarchical workload sieve' to produce safe, incremental batching and caching parameter suggestions. Implement a rollout controller called 'safe-splay' that staggers application...
text: Build a recommendation engine that profiles workloads at multiple granularities (route, model, customer cohort, temporal windows) using a 'hierarchical workload sieve' to produce safe, incremental batching and caching parameter suggestions. Implement a rollout controller called 'safe-splay' that staggers application across micro-cohorts with randomized splay windows, shadow verification, and automatic rollback when leading indicators exceed thresholds.

title: Backfill and migration defaults for existing Dedicated deployments (no behavior change until opt-in)
preview:
Context
We are adding scaling policy fields (min/max bounds + predictive settings) for Dedicated deployments. Existing customers must see zero behavioral change until explicitly enabled via feature flag + policy enablement. We also need auditability for enterprise: backfill audit trails so that compliance reviews ca...
text:
Context
We are adding scaling policy fields (min/max bounds + predictive settings) for Dedicated deployments. Existing customers must see zero behavioral change until explicitly enabled via feature flag + policy enablement. We also need auditability for enterprise: backfill audit trails so that compliance reviews can see when a deployment was migrated and what the effective default policy is.

Problem
If we add new fields with naive defaults (e.g., min=0, max=unbounded, predictive enabled/disabled), existing deployments could change scaling behavior (especially scale-down behavior). We also need deterministic "effective policy" evaluation and clear audit history.

Goals
1) No behavior changes for existing Dedicated deployments until enabled.
2) Safe, explicit defaults for new policy fields on existing rows/configs.
3) Backfill audit events to record migration/backfill actions (who/whe...
tags: Autoscaling policies with customer bounds (Dedicated Capacity & Fleet Management)

title: Emit audit events for Dedicated scaling policy changes and autoscaler decisions
preview:
Context
Dedicated customers are requesting contractual guardrails (min/max bounds) and predictable scaling. For enterprise and compliance-heavy accounts (e.g., Lumen Legaltech), we also need auditable, deterministic records of:
1) who changed scaling bounds / predictive settings (RBAC + change control), and
2) why t...
text:
Context
Dedicated customers are requesting contractual guardrails (min/max bounds) and predictable scaling. For enterprise and compliance-heavy accounts (e.g., Lumen Legaltech), we also need auditable, deterministic records of:
1) who changed scaling bounds / predictive settings (RBAC + change control), and
2) why the autoscaler chose a capacity (requested desired vs bounded desired), including conflicts between reactive vs predictive signals.

This ticket covers emitting audit events (not just metrics/logs) with defined retention and exportability requirements.

Goals / Requirements
A) Policy change audit events (control plane)
- Emit an audit event whenever a scaling policy is created/updated/deleted.
- Capture actor identity and auth context:
 - actor_type: user | service
 - actor_id (user_id or service principal)
 - actor_email (if user)
 - auth_mechanism: console | api_key | terr...
tags: Autoscaling policies with customer bounds (Dedicated Capacity & Fleet Management)

title: Add Dedicated scaling policy fields to control plane API (min/max bounds + predictive mode)
preview:
Context
Enterprise Dedicated customers want contractual/operational guardrails for scaling: hard min/max bounds and (optionally) predictive scaling. The fleet autoscaler can’t enforce any customer bounds until the control plane can persist, validate, and expose the policy via API/Console with proper RBAC + audit log...
text:
Context
Enterprise Dedicated customers want contractual/operational guardrails for scaling: hard min/max bounds and (optionally) predictive scaling. The fleet autoscaler can’t enforce any customer bounds until the control plane can persist, validate, and expose the policy via API/Console with proper RBAC + audit logging.

Goal
Add scaling policy fields to the Dedicated deployment config in the control plane, including migrations/backfill defaults, validation, RBAC, and audit logging hooks. Preserve existing behavior for all customers until explicitly enabled via feature flags.

Scope / Deliverables
1) Control plane schema changes
- Extend Dedicated deployment resource to include a scaling policy section (name tbd but should align with Console spec).
- Fields (v1; align with design spec + ADR):
 - enabled: boolean (default false / unset)
 - min_replicas: int (>= 0)
 - max_replicas: int...
tags: Autoscaling policies with customer bounds (Dedicated Capacity & Fleet Management)

text:
Context
Dedicated Burst Capacity requires a tenant-scoped configuration surface in the Control Plane so we can:
- enable/disable burst per Dedicated tenant
- set hard caps/guardrails that admission/routing can enforce deterministically
- expose read APIs to Console + support tooling
- produce audit events for all config changes (including admin overrides)

This ticket is specifically the Control Plane API + validation + audit logging hooks. The admission controller and routing integration are tracked separately.

Scope (in)
1) Tenant model/config schema
- Add a BurstConfig block on the tenant (or dedicated capacity config) with:
 - enabled: boolean
 - max_burst_ratio: float (relative to baseline reserved capacity) OR max_burst_gpus: int (absolute cap), with clear precedence rules
 - max_burst_gpus: int (optional)
 - priority_tier: enum (e.g., standard | silver | gold) used only for fa...

title: Filtering engine for SIEM-ready audit exports (allow/deny by category/action + actor/resource predicates)
preview:
Goal
Implement the filtering model for SIEM-ready audit exports to support enterprise requirements (noise reduction, least-data export) while preserving delivery guarantees and avoiding performance regressions.

This ticket covers:
- Runtime evaluation of export filters (exporter-side) for each audit event.
- Valida...
text:
Goal
Implement the filtering model for SIEM-ready audit exports to support enterprise requirements (noise reduction, least-data export) while preserving delivery guarantees and avoiding performance regressions.

This ticket covers:
- Runtime evaluation of export filters (exporter-side) for each audit event.
- Validation/normalization rules shared with control plane to prevent unsafe/expensive filters.
- Safe defaults and clear semantics (allowlist + denylist, deny wins).
- Performance tests to ensure filtering overhead is negligible at expected event volumes.

References
- Confluence ADR: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-audit-export-filtering-model.json
- Schema v2 + category taxonomy: sources/confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/audit-event-schema-v2-and-siem-mapping.json
- Control plane export config API ticket: ENG-5132
- Exporter scaf...
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation — SIEM-ready audit export (Beta)

Designs must be handoff-ready for engineering with responsive breakpoints, exportable assets, and annotated motion specs.

title: Control plane: annotate sensitive endpoints with RBAC v2 permissions + add coverage gate
preview:
## Context
RBAC v2 requires deny-by-default authorization enforcement across Gateway + Control Plane services. Gateway middleware (ENG-18277) will block requests unless the route is mapped to a permission and the request is authorized.

We are currently missing consistent permission annotations on several Control Pl...
text:
## Context
RBAC v2 requires deny-by-default authorization enforcement across Gateway + Control Plane services. Gateway middleware (ENG-18277) will block requests unless the route is mapped to a permission and the request is authorized.

We are currently missing consistent permission annotations on several Control Plane endpoints (org/user/role management, routing policy updates, model pinning/rollout config, audit export controls, private deployment admin ops). This creates two risks:
1) accidental implicit allow (or inconsistent allow) when checks are skipped
2) drift over time: new endpoints ship without annotations/permission mapping

This ticket covers Control Plane endpoint annotation + a test/CI gate to ensure no unannotated *sensitive* endpoints ship.

## Goals
- Every security-relevant Control Plane endpoint has an explicit RBAC v2 permission annotation.
- Enforcement path is ...

title: Debug Composer: Incident Playbook & Payload Lenses (Prototype & Review)
preview: Design a consolidated console surface called "Debug Composer" that combines an incident playbook, interactive payload lenses, and retry orchestration controls. The goal is to reduce mean time to triage (MTTT) for failed requests by putting contextualized debugging actions and structured guidance in one place. This t...
text: Design a consolidated console surface called "Debug Composer" that combines an incident playbook, interactive payload lenses, and retry orchestration controls. The goal is to reduce mean time to triage (MTTT) for failed requests by putting contextualized debugging actions and structured guidance in one place. This ticket covers the high-fidelity prototype, annotated interaction specs, and a short internal review session with PM/Eng for alignment. Scope includes: failed request list -> composer panel, payload lenses (raw, delta, KV-cache timeline), retry orchestration bar, error taxonomy tagger, and cost/latency overlays tied to token usage. Scope excludes backend API changes (handled by ENG) and long-term storage/retention policies.

title: Consolidate SIG/CAIQ responses and standardized customer attestations for pricing tiers
preview: Goal: Create a single authoritative SIG/CAIQ response bundle and a set of tiered customer attestation templates that map to Redwood pricing/packaging tiers. This reduces sales cycles, ensures consistent legal posture across deals, and enables automated artifact generation for self-serve and sales-assisted flows. Sco...
text: Goal: Create a single authoritative SIG/CAIQ response bundle and a set of tiered customer attestation templates that map to Redwood pricing/packaging tiers. This reduces sales cycles, ensures consistent legal posture across deals, and enables automated artifact generation for self-serve and sales-assisted flows. Scope: inventory existing responses, reconcile conflicts between engineering artifacts and legal guidance, produce a canonical CAIQ answer pack (v1) plus three attestation templates (Self-Serve, Dedicated, Private) with agreed scoping language. Deliverables: canonical CAIQ JSON+PDF, three attestation templates, a lightweight sales playbook page, a generator script to inject customer-specific values, and a legal sign-off checklist.

text:

title: KV soft-tiering: TTL+LRU hybrid with prefix-aware safety and serializability sketch
preview: Design and implement a 'soft-tier' eviction policy that combines TTL windows and LRU scoring with explicit prefix-awareness and a set of lightweight correctness guards. Goal: reduce pathological eviction cascades during continuous batching and prefetch while preserving strong observable guarantees (serializability o...
text: Design and implement a 'soft-tier' eviction policy that combines TTL windows and LRU scoring with explicit prefix-awareness and a set of lightweight correctness guards. Goal: reduce pathological eviction cascades during continuous batching and prefetch while preserving strong observable guarantees (serializability of cache effects w.r.t. requests) and keeping tail-latency/regressions within performance SLOs.

title: Hosted API cascade insulation and rollback safepoints for customer escalations
preview: Summary: Several high-severity customer escalations in the past quarter revealed a repeating pattern: tenant burst -> edge/backplane retries -> downstream queueing -> KV cache eviction -> amplified 5xx/timeouts that impact non-offending tenants. This ticket implements a multi-layered mitigation: (1) escalation gates...
text: Summary: Several high-severity customer escalations in the past quarter revealed a repeating pattern: tenant burst -> edge/backplane retries -> downstream queueing -> KV cache eviction -> amplified 5xx/timeouts that impact non-offending tenants. This ticket implements a multi-layered mitigation: (1) escalation gates (a short-lived, per-tenant fast-fail / priority path for customers with open escalations), (2) differential retry budgets and jittered backoffs at the edge, and (3) observable rollback safepoints and rollout gates so we can rapidly revert or restrict new routing logic without taking the entire fleet offline. The goal is to stop blast-radius amplification while preserving SLOs for paid/enterprise tenants and maintain graceful degradation for lower tiers.

title: Spectral Tint Blend and Tokenized Interaction Scale for Charts & Controls
preview: Problem: Chart palettes and component tints currently degrade when interpolated across light/dark surfaces; border and micro-shadow tokens are inconsistent for compact controls which causes perceived jitter in dense tables. Goal: introduce a spectral-aware tint blending algorithm and a small set of new semantic toke...
text: Problem: Chart palettes and component tints currently degrade when interpolated across light/dark surfaces; border and micro-shadow tokens are inconsistent for compact controls which causes perceived jitter in dense tables. Goal: introduce a spectral-aware tint blending algorithm and a small set of new semantic tokens (Tint.* and MicroRadius.*) that produce perceptually uniform steps across color ramps and responsive radii for compact components. Deliverables: token spec (JSON + migration guide), updated Figma components, component PRs for Button/Table/Chart tokens, cross-theme QA matrix, and a lightweight migration script for consumers. Non-goal: changing visual language or color semantics for product teams — we aim to preserve existing brand anchors while improving interpolation behavior and small-radius consistency.

title: TTL enforcement + purge job for request/response logs store
preview:
Goal
Implement TTL enforcement (scheduled purge) for the request/response logs data store, driven by the retention policy evaluation result for each tenant. Must be idempotent, observable, and safe under peak load.

Context
This is part of the Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation (“Retention controls + compli...
text:
Goal
Implement TTL enforcement (scheduled purge) for the request/response logs data store, driven by the retention policy evaluation result for each tenant. Must be idempotent, observable, and safe under peak load.

Context
This is part of the Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation (“Retention controls + compliance reporting pack”). Request/response logs are one of the primary data classes called out in security reviews; we need predictable deletion semantics and evidence (metrics + exports) that retention is being applied.

In-scope
- Add TTL/purge enforcement for request/response logs store ("request_logs" service DB/table/collection).
- Wire purge behavior to evaluated retention policy (per tenant, per region where applicable).
- Support safe backfills (existing rows) and ongoing incremental purging.
- Emit verification metrics and hook into standard dashboards/alerts.

Non-go...

title: Implement routing policy: map Dedicated tier → hardware pool/shape selection
preview:
We need a deterministic and auditable mapping from customer Dedicated tier (Standard/Plus/Premium) to eligible hardware pools/shapes in the router/orchestration layer.

This is required to:
- enforce tier-specific throughput floors by ensuring requests land on the right capacity class
- preserve Dedicated isolation ...
text:
We need a deterministic and auditable mapping from customer Dedicated tier (Standard/Plus/Premium) to eligible hardware pools/shapes in the router/orchestration layer.

This is required to:
- enforce tier-specific throughput floors by ensuring requests land on the right capacity class
- preserve Dedicated isolation semantics while still allowing defined burst/failover behaviors
- make capacity accounting and Sales quoting consistent (tier → shape families per region)

Scope:
1) Add tier-aware pool selection in routing policy for Dedicated deployments.
2) Wire to control-plane config so policy can be changed without redeploying the router.
3) Implement safe defaults + feature-flag rollout.
4) Emit routing decision telemetry (selected_pool, reason, fallbacks) to support SLO debugging.

Non-goals:
- Designing the final shape catalog (owned by Infra)
- Implementing admission control/overl...

title: Quant artifact anchoring and rollback ledger for reproducible calibration
preview: Problem: We currently produce FP8/INT8 quant artifacts and calibration snapshots without a single authoritative, signed record that binds the artifact bytes to the calibration inputs, quantization config, toolchain, and model binary. That makes retrospective verification, reproducible rollbacks, and automated safety...
text:
Problem: We currently produce FP8/INT8 quant artifacts and calibration snapshots without a single authoritative, signed record that binds the artifact bytes to the calibration inputs, quantization config, toolchain, and model binary. That makes retrospective verification, reproducible rollbacks, and automated safety checks brittle.

Goal: Implement an artifact anchoring ledger and CLI verification flow that: (1) computes deterministic content fingerprints for model binaries, calibration datasets, quant profiles and build metadata; (2) publishes a signed, append-only ledger record per quant release containing the fingerprints, human-readable manifest, and storage pointers; (3) provides a verification tool to replay/validate calibration results and detect bit-rot or untrusted modifications; (4) surface an automated rollback playbook when verification or accuracy validation fails.

Succe...

title: Config Safety Net: causal-trace rollback, transient patching, and economic rebalancer
preview: Deliver an automated safety net for config changes that (1) identifies the causal config change for regressions using causal tracing, (2) applies a transient patch rollback that isolates the faulty change without impacting unrelated routes, and (3) triggers an "economic rebalancer" that temporarily adjusts batching/...
text: Deliver an automated safety net for config changes that (1) identifies the causal config change for regressions using causal tracing, (2) applies a transient patch rollback that isolates the faulty change without impacting unrelated routes, and (3) triggers an "economic rebalancer" that temporarily adjusts batching/caching knobs to limit cost exposure while preserving latency SLOs.

title: Payload sparsifier & run-length microbatcher with kernel-cost evaluator for prefill/decode trimming
preview:
Problem: At 99th-percentile load we see wasted token compute from padding-heavy micro-batches where a small fraction of long sequences force larger kernel selection and reduce packing density. This increases per-token cost and occasionally pushes decode latency over our SLOs when kernel switching occurs mid-decode.
...
text:
Problem: At 99th-percentile load we see wasted token compute from padding-heavy micro-batches where a small fraction of long sequences force larger kernel selection and reduce packing density. This increases per-token cost and occasionally pushes decode latency over our SLOs when kernel switching occurs mid-decode.

Goal: Implement a microbatch shaping component (Payload Sparsifier + Run-Length Batcher) that dynamically trims and groups prefills/decodes by runtime-observed payload patterns and a kernel-cost evaluator. The component should: (1) reduce padded tokens by opportunistic run-length trimming and prefix concatenation where semantics allow, (2) choose kernel variants based on a cost model (latency vs compute) and predicted sequence-length distribution, (3) schedule prefill and decode phases to avoid expensive kernel swaps during time-critical decode windows, and (4) expose safe...

title: SAML SSO: IdP-specific integration playbook for RBAC v2, audit logging, and admin controls
preview: Consolidate IdP-specific compatibility notes, verification steps, and mitigation patterns for Okta, Azure AD (Entra), Google Workspace, and Ping Identity with respect to RBAC v2, enhanced audit logging, and admin control surfaces. This ticket produces a playbook for engineering, an integration checklist for solution...
text: Consolidate IdP-specific compatibility notes, verification steps, and mitigation patterns for Okta, Azure AD (Entra), Google Workspace, and Ping Identity with respect to RBAC v2, enhanced audit logging, and admin control surfaces. This ticket produces a playbook for engineering, an integration checklist for solutions engineering, and a test-suite matrix for QA. The goal is to reduce onboarding friction for enterprise customers and close known swallow/attribute-mapping gaps that cause admin/control regressions and audit inconsistencies.

title: Rotation scoping mismatch causing privilege escalation between orgs during token refresh
preview: Multiple customers reported unexpected cross-organization access following API key rotations and token refreshes in Dedicated and Private deployments. Investigation shows that under a rare combination of rolling rotations and scaled routing, the token refresh endpoint returned a session token scoped incorrectly (mis...
text: Multiple customers reported unexpected cross-organization access following API key rotations and token refreshes in Dedicated and Private deployments. Investigation shows that under a rare combination of rolling rotations and scaled routing, the token refresh endpoint returned a session token scoped incorrectly (missing tenant binding), allowing a refreshed token to be used against a different org's resources. Hosted deployments are largely unaffected due to a separate auth proxy that enforces org headers; the issue appears in our self-hosted auth-service path used by Dedicated/Private customers. This ticket tracks the immediate remediation, root cause fix, test coverage, rollout, and customer communications.

title: Mini-carousel quickstart: compact hosted-API screenshots + code-toggle
preview: Goal: produce a compact, mobile-first set of Hosted API getting-started screenshots for the Quickstart micro-tour. Asset set should demonstrate: model selector microflow, inline request builder (prompt presets + param sliders), code-snippet toggle (Python/Node/cURL), token-cost preview overlay, and accessibility alt...
text:
Goal: produce a compact, mobile-first set of Hosted API getting-started screenshots for the Quickstart micro-tour. Asset set should demonstrate: model selector microflow, inline request builder (prompt presets + param sliders), code-snippet toggle (Python/Node/cURL), token-cost preview overlay, and accessibility alt-text examples. Deliverables will be a carousel of 6 hero screens (desktop + mobile variants), export-ready PNGs and an associated code snippet text bundle for docs. Focus on clarity for first-time engineers and product-led sign-up flow.

Key design constraints: compact layout that fits 640x880 (mobile) and 1280x720 (web) hero slots; code snippet lines limited to 8 for thumbnails; visible cost-estimate pill (tokens/$) in top-right; accessible color contrast >= 4.5:1; keyboard focus states shown for model selector.

Acceptance criteria:
- 6 unique frames covering: (1) Welcom...
